channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Gambian torture victims testify in US against a member of former dictator's military
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Suspected of backing a coup plot against the longtime dictator of Gambia nearly 20 years ago, Pharing Sanyang described Thursday how he was beaten with pipes and palm tree branches, pistol-whipped and hit in the face with a hammer.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump nominee for public lands post withdraws after her criticism of Jan. 6 attack surfaces
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to oversee an agency that manages a quarter-billion acres of public land has withdrawn her nomination following revelations that she criticized the Republican president in 2021 for inciting the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's tariffs threaten to end quarter-century era of cheap goods for U.S. consumers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s new tariffs threaten to push up prices on clothes, mobile phones, furniture and many other products in the coming months, possibly ending the era of cheap goods that Americans enjoyed for about a quarter-century before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 argued some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a hearing Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: LA County homeless spending under the microscope of newly appointed US attorney
item.description: Newly appointed U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli has launched a federal task force to investigate billions in unaccounted homeless funds in Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Buyer of 'Full House' home to inherit celeb souvenirs
item.description: The San Francisco house that once belonged to the fictional Tanner family has sold after almost a year on the market.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, NewsNation

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House passes bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed a bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in a move that would vastly diminish the ability of courts to block President Trump’s policies. Dubbed the No Rogue Rulings Act, the legislation from Rep. Darrell Issa (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: VP of Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter arrested for sex crimes against children
item.description: He was charged with promoting a sexual performance by a child and possessing a sexual performance by a child.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Court of Appeals rejects petition to dismiss indictment of ex-congressional candidate accused of murder
item.description: The Nevada Court of Appeals Wednesday rejected a petition to dismiss the indictment against a former Republican congressional candidate and ex-wrestler charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sam's Club plans to open 15 new locations a year, CEO says
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the company plans to open 15 new locations a year "for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Woman can testify Weinstein used force to rape her despite his acquittal, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A woman who says Harvey Weinstein trapped her in a Manhattan hotel room and raped her in 2013 can use the word “force” when she testifies at his retrial even though the disgraced movie mogul was acquitted of using physical force against her, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp to 78 cents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is seeking a rate increase this summer that includes hiking the cost of a first-class stamp from 73 cents to 78 cents. The request was made Wednesday to the Postal Regulatory Commission, which must OK the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dramatic video captures firefighter being pulled from rubble after home explosion
item.description: Dramatic body camera footage captured the moments an Illinois home exploded, trapping a firefighter underneath the rubble.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Group calls for investigation into Ms. Rachel over posts about Gaza children
item.description: Rachel Griffin Accurso, who goes by Ms. Rachel to her more than 14 million subscribers on YouTube, makes videos for children.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, KRON4

item.title: Kennedy’s moves on measles don't quell criticism
item.description: The outbreak represents the first time Kennedy has had to reconcile his past as a leading critic of vaccines and his leadership of the federal health establishment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariffs, including massive levies on China, go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump's latest round of sweeping reciprocal tariffs went into effect early Wednesday, including a 104 percent import tax on China.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: New tariffs take effect
item.description: President Donald Trump's latest round of tariffs is now in effect and concerns from Capitol Hill are growing louder, even among Republican lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Brandt, Fox 5

item.title: International students at several Utah universities had their visas revoked
item.description: On Tuesday, a spokesperson for the University of Utah confirmed that about 17 international students or graduates have had their visas revoked as of April 8.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah woman who survived being shot in the head as a teenager now in hospice
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now on hospice after recent health complications.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Murphy, ABC 4

item.title: Judge rules in favor of AP in 'Gulf of America' spat
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to restore the Associated Press's full media access, which was suspended after the news organization said it would not follow Trump's executive order to refer to the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Keystone oil pipeline shutdown could quickly lead to higher gasoline prices
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — The nearly 2,700-mile Keystone oil pipeline was shut down Tuesday morning after it ruptured in North Dakota, halting the flow of millions of gallons of crude oil from Canada to refineries in the U.S. and potentially leading to higher gasoline prices.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spring skiing still thriving at Snowbird as Utah ski season winds down
item.description: Spring skiing remains in full swing at Snowbird, where skiers continue to take advantage of excellent conditions as the season enters its final stretch.
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: Arroyo Colorado to be dredged after historic Rio Grande Valley floods
item.description: A Colorado company will design the dredging of a section of a South Texas waterway that flooded during historic recent rains, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commission says.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Italy fines Roman Colosseum ticketing agencies $22M for ripping off tourists
item.description: Italy is levying hefty fines against Roman Colosseum ticketing agencies for forcing tourists to pay more to visit the famed historic site.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Chan, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit is on trial in the US for torture
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A man accused of torturing people suspected in a planned coup against Gambia’s longtime leader was a low-ranking private in the West African country’s military who risked torture and death himself if he disobeyed superiors, a lawyer for the defendant told jurors Tuesday in opening stat
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rapper 'Fat Flow' gets 33 years for kidnapping migrants
item.description: A judge has sentenced a rapper calling himself Fat Flow to 33 years and four months in prison for the 2023 kidnapping of seven migrants in Juarez, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration cancels $188M in grants New York City was using to shelter migrants
item.description: The Trump administration canceled $188 million in federal grants meant to reimburse New York City for sheltering migrants, saying the money was being spent to support illegal immigration and leading the city's mayor to vow to fight the clawback.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California man arrested near Justice Kavanaugh's home with a gun pleads guilty to attempted murder
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A California man pleaded guilty on Tuesday to trying to kill U.S. Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh at his home in a suburb of Washington, D.C., nearly three years ago. Nicholas John Roske was arrested near Kavanaugh’s home in Chevy Chase, Maryland, in June 2022.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
